2605.12412.title: Stories in Space: In-Context Learning Trajectories in Conceptual Belief Space
2605.12412.paper_summary: Large Language Models (LLMs) update their behavior in context, which can be viewed as a form of Bayesian inference. However, the structure of the latent hypothesis space over which this inference operates remains unclear. In this work, we propose that LLMs assign beliefs over a low-dimensional geometric space - a conceptual belief space - and that in-context learning corresponds to a trajectory through this space as beliefs are updated over time. Using story understanding as a natural setting for dynamic belief updating, we combine behavioral and representational analyses to study these trajectories. We find that (1) belief updates are well-described as trajectories on low-dimensional, structured manifolds; (2) this structure is reflected consistently in both model behavior and internal representations and can be decoded with simple linear probes to predict behavior; and (3) interventions on these representations causally steer belief trajectories, with effects that can be predicted from the geometry of the conceptual space. Together, our results provide a geometric account of belief dynamics in LLMs, grounding Bayesian interpretations of in-context learning in structured conceptual representations.
2605.12412.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）在上下文中更新其行為，這可以視為一種貝葉斯推斷。<lb/>然而，這種推斷所操作的潛在假設空間的結構仍然不清楚。<lb/>在這項工作中，我們提出LLMs在一個低維幾何空間中分配信念——一個概念信念空間——並且上下文學習對應於隨著時間推移信念更新的這個空間中的軌跡。<lb/>利用故事理解作為動態信念更新的自然環境，我們結合行為和表徵分析來研究這些軌跡。<lb/>我們發現（1）信念更新可以很好地描述為低維結構流形上的軌跡；（2）這種結構在模型行為和內部表徵中一致地反映出來，並且可以通過簡單的線性探針解碼以預測行為；以及（3）對這些表徵的干預會因果地引導信念軌跡，其效果可以從概念空間的幾何形狀中預測。<lb/>總體而言，我們的結果提供了一種幾何學的信念動態解釋，將貝葉斯對上下文學習的解釋根植於結構化的概念表徵中。
2605.12412.author: Eric Bigelow et.al.
2605.12412.authors: Eric Bigelow, Raphaël Sarfati, Daniel Wurgaft, Owen Lewis, Thomas McGrath, Jack Merullo, Atticus Geiger, Ekdeep Singh Lubana
